heaviest_rainfall.status: On 09 November 2025, a record rainfall of 47.89mm was measured, indicating an exceptionally wet day.  24.6mm per hour was recorded as falling at times during the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: A torrential downpour occurred on 02 September 2025 when the rain reached a maximum intensity of 34.3mm per hour.  This happened during a 15.0 hour storm when 21.19mm of rain fell.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: There are no active storms in the area.  The previous storm started on 21 December 2025 and lasted 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: A significant dry period is on-going and is ranked 7th for length since 23 September 2024.  So far, it has lasted 9 consecutive days beginning 23 December 2025.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not a cold snap right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C) on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and it has only happened 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (196°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 9.9°C), outside temperature (down 7.3°C), and feels like temperature (down 6.6°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 126 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 208 m), inside temperature (up 8.9°C), and inside humidity (up 7%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 125.9m lower than yesterday, 208.0m lower than the past week, 187.2m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 828m minimum and 2280m maximum, with an average of 1155.0m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 1363.0m and 967.8m.

summary.summary: Inside; anticipate brisk, cold temperatures. Outside; freezing temperatures are anticipated. Also, very little to no wind will be present. Chance of light rain, occasional overcast conditions, and it’s dark. Weather should be calm and steady.

washing.phrase: It is nighttime; if you have washing out then bring it in as it will probably get damper overnight.
